title: Metabolomic Analysis of Polyamines
author: Sarah van Veen
materials: Sample preparation - biological fluids and tissue

steps.title: Wash cells with 0
steps.instruction: Wash cells with 0.9% NaCl.

steps.title: Collect the supernatant, flash-freeze in liquid...
steps.instruction: Collect the supernatant, flash-freeze in liquid nitrogen, and store at -80 °C .

steps.title: Centrifuge lysate at 20000 x g, 4 °C, 20 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge lysate at 20000 x g, 4 °C, 20 minutes .

steps.title: Collect lysate with a cell scraper...
steps.instruction: Collect lysate with a cell scraper and incubate On ice for 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Add 150 µL of ice-cold 6% TCA to extract metabolites
steps.instruction: Add 150 µL of ice-cold 6% TCA to extract metabolites.

steps.title: Add 25 µL of isobutyl chloroformate,...
steps.instruction: Add 25 µL of isobutyl chloroformate, incubate at 35 °C for 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Add 900 µL of sodium carbonate...
steps.instruction: Add 900 µL of sodium carbonate buffer to 100 µL of supernatant.

steps.title: Collect 900 µL of the upper...
steps.instruction: Collect 900 µL of the upper phase, dry under vacuum, and dissolve in 125 µL of 50% acetonitrile in water with 0.1% formic acid.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of diethyl ether,...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of diethyl ether, vortex, and incubate for 00:15:00 at 25 °C .

steps.title: Transfer 800 µL of the reaction...
steps.instruction: Transfer 800 µL of the reaction mixture to a 2 mL Eppendorf tube.

steps.title: Apply a gradient
steps.instruction: Apply a gradient: - 0–2 min: 20% B - 2–10 min: Linear increase to 85% B - 10–17 min: Hold at 85% B - 18 min: Return to 20% B

steps.title: Use solvent A (H 2 O with 0
steps.instruction: Use solvent A (H 2 O with 0.2% acetic acid) and solvent B (acetonitrile with 0.2% acetic acid).

steps.title: Load 10 µL onto the LC...
steps.instruction: Load 10 µL onto the LC system with an ACQUITY UPLC HSS C18 column.

steps.title: Set mass spectrometer to full scan (70–750 m/z), positive mode
steps.instruction: Set mass spectrometer to full scan (70–750 m/z), positive mode.

steps.title: Maintain flow rate at 250 μL/min...
steps.instruction: Maintain flow rate at 250 μL/min and column temperature at 30 °C .

steps.title: Transfer to an MS vial
steps.instruction: Transfer to an MS vial.

steps.title: Apply a gradient
steps.instruction: Apply a gradient: - 0–0.67 min: 5% B, flow rate 1.8 mL/min to waste - 0.67–1 min: 5–35% B - 1–1.1 min: Flow reduced to 0.3 mL/min, increase to 36% B - 1.1–3 min: Linear increase to 85% B, hold until 4.7 min - 4.7–4.73 min: Redirect flow to waste, decrease flow to 1.8 mL/min - 4.73–4.86 min: Decrease to 5% B, equilibrate

steps.title: Load 50 µL onto the LC...
steps.instruction: Load 50 µL onto the LC system with a VanGuard Pre-column.

steps.title: Transfer to an MS vial
steps.instruction: Transfer to an MS vial.

steps.title: Incubate at 35 °C for 00:30:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 35 °C for 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Add 360 µL of sodium carbonate...
steps.instruction: Add 360 µL of sodium carbonate buffer and 10 µL of isobutyl chloroformate.

steps.title: Centrifuge and collect 40 µL of supernatant for derivatization
steps.instruction: Centrifuge and collect 40 µL of supernatant for derivatization.

steps.title: Mix 50 µL of ACM or...
steps.instruction: Mix 50 µL of ACM or plasma with 50 µL of ice-cold TCA extraction buffer containing labeled internal standards. Internal standards: 13 C 5 ornithine (Cat# CLM-4724-H-PK), 13 C 4 putrescine (Cat# CLM-6574-PK), 13 C 4 spermidine (Cat# CLM-9435-PK) and D 8 spermine (Cat# DLM-9262-PK) from Cambridge Isotope Laboratories, Inc.

steps.title: For cerebrospinal fluid (CSF), combine each...
steps.instruction: For cerebrospinal fluid (CSF), combine each sample with an equal volume of ice-cold 6% TCA extraction buffer containing labeled internal standards. Due to the limited volume obtained per animal, a subset of CSF samples may need to be pooled prior to extraction to ensure sufficient material for LC-MS/MS analysis. Pool samples across animals of the same genotype and sex. Pooled samples are processed identically to individual samples. In figures, represent individual samples as filled symbols and pooled samples as open symbols.

steps.title: For cortical brain tissue (15–30 mg),...
steps.instruction: For cortical brain tissue (15–30 mg), add 250 µL of ice-cold TCA extraction buffer containing labeled internal standards and homogenize samples in Ribolyser tubes to ensure thorough extraction.
